full_response:
Let [M] and [N] be two 3x3 square matrices. Their entries are denoted by $M_{ij}$ and $N_{ij}$ respectively, where $i$ represents the row index and $j$ represents the column index, both ranging from 1 to 3.

We are asked to write down the matrix product [N]·[M] using the Σ sum notation.
Let the resulting product matrix be [P], such that [P] = [N]·[M].

According to the rules of matrix multiplication, an entry $P_{ik}$ in the $i$-th row and $k$-th column of the product matrix [P] is calculated by taking the dot product of the $i$-th row of matrix [N] and the $k$-th column of matrix [M].

For 3x3 matrices, this means that for each entry $P_{ik}$:
$P_{ik} = N_{i1}M_{1k} + N_{i2}M_{2k} + N_{i3}M_{3k}$

Using the Σ sum notation, this can be written concisely as:
$P_{ik} = \sum_{j=1}^{3} N_{ij} M_{jk}$

This formula applies for all $i, k \in \{1, 2, 3\}$.

The final product matrix [P] can therefore be represented by its entries as:
$[P] = \begin{pmatrix}
\sum_{j=1}^{3} N_{1j} M_{j1} & \sum_{j=1}^{3} N_{1j} M_{j2} & \sum_{j=1}^{3} N_{1j} M_{j3} \\
\sum_{j=1}^{3} N_{2j} M_{j1} & \sum_{j=1}^{3} N_{2j} M_{j2} & \sum_{j=1}^{3} N_{2j} M_{j3} \\
\sum_{j=1}^{3} N_{3j} M_{j1} & \sum_{j=1}^{3} N_{3j} M_{j2} & \sum_{j=1}^{3} N_{3j} M_{j3}
\end{pmatrix}$

The problem asks to write down [N]·[M] using the Σ sum notation. This implies providing the general form for the elements of the product matrix.

\boxed{The entries of the product matrix [P] = [N]·[M], denoted as $P_{ik}$, are given by:
$P_{ik} = \sum_{j=1}^{3} N_{ij} M_{jk}$}
Confidence: 100%
